frameworks.name: Pavlovian-instrumental arbitration
frameworks.one_line: controllability inference governs the weighting of Pavlovian versus instrumental action selection

frameworks.name: inferred controllability
frameworks.one_line: a computational signal modulating how much Pavlovian biases drive behavior

frameworks.name: model-based behavioral analysis
frameworks.one_line: fit a computational arbitration model to Go/NoGo choices and neural signals

frameworks.name: frontal theta oscillations
frameworks.one_line: frontal theta-band power as a neural correlate of inferred control

core_question: How does the brain arbitrate between Pavlovian and instrumental influences on action selection?
key_assumptions: Pavlovian and instrumental systems contribute jointly to action selection
inferred controllability over outcomes adjusts their relative weighting
controllability can be inferred online from task structure
manipulations: Go/NoGo task with congruent and conflicting Pavlovian cues
conditions varying inferred controllability of outcomes
key_findings: frontal theta-band oscillatory power tracks inferred controllability
inferred controllability predicts the strength of Pavlovian action biases
inferior frontal gyrus activity correlates with action probability
ventromedial prefrontal activity correlates with outcome valence, modulated by controllability
interpretations: frontal theta indexes a controllability signal arbitrating Pavlovian vs instrumental control
prefrontal value and action signals are gated by inferred controllability
